name: Calendly API
what_it_does: Scheduling platform API for reading user availability, managing event types, retrieving scheduled meetings, and processing booking data.
use_cases: Retrieving scheduled meetings for CRM sync from agents
Monitoring new bookings via webhooks for follow-up automation
Getting user availability for scheduling coordination
Generating custom booking links with pre-filled data
not_for: Creating new booking slots (Calendly doesn't support this via API)
Teams not using Calendly for scheduling
best_when: An agent needs to react to new bookings, sync calendar data to CRM, or process scheduling events from users already on Calendly.
avoid_when: You need to fully automate booking creation or manage complex resource scheduling.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens for single-user agents. OAuth2 for multi-user apps. PATs available on paid plans only.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free basic scheduling with 1 event type, no API access
pricing.notes: API access requires paid plan. Free tier has no API access which limits agent development.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST errors. Documentation is functional but less comprehensive than Google Calendar.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-heavy API. Few write operations exist.
known_agent_gotchas: API is mostly read-only - cannot create event types or booking pages via API
Paid plan required for API access - no free tier API for development
No MCP server - REST API only
Webhook payloads require payload signature verification
Organization vs user scope matters for multi-user accounts
